steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says respondents were "more likely ______ common Brazilian foods." After the adjective phrase more likely, Standard English requires an infinitive (to + base verb) to complete the construction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'eaten': Past participle. "More likely eaten" does not form a grammatical or idiomatic construction in this context. ✗
  • Choice B — 'to eat': Infinitive. "More likely to eat" is the standard form after a likelihood adjective in English. ✓
  • Choice C — 'ate': Simple past tense. A finite past-tense verb cannot follow likely here; it would require "that they ate," which changes the sentence structure entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — 'will be eating': Future progressive. A finite verb form cannot directly follow likely without introducing a clause with that. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
